test_plan.scope: Unit, integration, and e2e tests for the integration-last-reviewed-gate feature: version gate logic, feature sub-pipeline aggregation, CLI and web rendering, dashboard.json schema extension
out_of_scope: Tests for existing FR-001 through FR-009 behavior (covered by parent project test suite)
test_environments: Node.js 18+ on macOS/Linux using os.tmpdir() for filesystem fixtures
React component tests using @testing-library/react with jsdom
test_plan.automation_framework: Node.js built-in test runner (node:test) for CLI unit/integration; Vitest + @testing-library/react for web components

test_cases.title: Version gate generates warning alert when detected version exceeds INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED
test_cases.given: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.76' in constants.js and detectedVersion = '0.1.77'
test_cases.then: returns { severity: 'warning', message: 'Aitri 0.1.77 detected — Hub integration not reviewed past 0.1.76', changelogUrl: 'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/blob/main/docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md' }
test_cases.expected_result: Object with severity='warning', message containing both version strings '0.1.77' and '0.1.76', non-empty changelogUrl

test_cases.title: Version gate returns null when detected version equals INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED
test_cases.given: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.76' and detectedVersion = '0.1.76'
test_cases.then: returns null
test_cases.expected_result: null — no alert generated; strict equality is not a mismatch

test_cases.title: Version gate returns null when detected version is older than INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED
test_cases.given: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.76' and detectedVersion = '0.1.70'
test_cases.then: returns null
test_cases.expected_result: null — older CLI is not a mismatch; Hub is ahead of the installed CLI

test_cases.title: Version gate generates 'undetectable' warning when detectedVersion is null
test_cases.given: INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.76' and detectedVersion = null
test_cases.then: returns { severity: 'warning', message: 'Aitri CLI version undetectable — integration status unknown', changelogUrl: non-empty string }
test_cases.expected_result: Object with severity='warning'; message contains 'undetectable'; changelogUrl is non-empty

test_cases.title: Feature reader extracts phase data and TC count from a single valid feature
test_cases.given: A temp project dir containing: features/auth/.aitri = {approvedPhases:[1,2], currentPhase:3, verifyPassed:false} and features/auth/spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json = {summary:{passed:30,failed:0,total:61}}; main TC total = 30
test_cases.then: returns { featurePipelines: [{name:'auth', approvedPhases:[1,2], currentPhase:3, totalPhases:5, tcCount:61, verifyStatus:{passed:false}}], aggregatedTcTotal: 91 }
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines array length = 1; featurePipelines[0].name = 'auth'; featurePipelines[0].tcCount = 61; aggregatedTcTotal = 91

test_cases.title: Feature reader aggregates TC counts from two features and sums with main
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with two features: features/auth/spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json {summary:{total:61}} and features/payments/spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json {summary:{total:20}}; both have valid .aitri files; mainTcTotal = 30
test_cases.then: returns aggregatedTcTotal = 111 (30 + 61 + 20) and featurePipelines array of length 2 containing entries for 'auth' and 'payments'
test_cases.expected_result: aggregatedTcTotal = 111; featurePipelines.length = 2; featurePipelines map to names ['auth', 'payments'] (order may vary)

test_cases.title: Feature reader returns empty array and unmodified mainTcTotal when features/ dir is absent
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with no features/ directory; mainTcTotal = 30
test_cases.then: returns { featurePipelines: [], aggregatedTcTotal: 30 } with no error thrown
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines = []; aggregatedTcTotal = 30; no exception thrown; no log entry written

test_cases.title: Feature reader sets tcCount=0 for a feature missing 04_TEST_RESULTS.json
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with features/no-tests/.aitri = {approvedPhases:[1], currentPhase:2} but no spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json; mainTcTotal = 30
test_cases.then: returns featurePipelines[0].tcCount = 0 and aggregatedTcTotal = 30 (30 + 0)
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines[0].name = 'no-tests'; featurePipelines[0].tcCount = 0; aggregatedTcTotal = 30; no exception thrown

test_cases.title: Feature reader skips subdirectory with no .aitri file without affecting valid features
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with features/valid-feat/.aitri = {approvedPhases:[1,2,3], currentPhase:4} and features/invalid-dir/ (no .aitri file); mainTcTotal = 10
test_cases.then: returns featurePipelines array of length 1 containing only 'valid-feat'; 'invalid-dir' is absent from results; no exception thrown
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines.length = 1; featurePipelines[0].name = 'valid-feat'; no error thrown; WARN log entry for 'invalid-dir' skipped is optional

test_cases.title: Feature reader handles malformed .aitri JSON gracefully
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with features/broken/.aitri containing '{invalid json}'; mainTcTotal = 5
test_cases.then: returns { featurePipelines: [], aggregatedTcTotal: 5 }; a WARN entry containing 'broken' is written to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log; no exception propagates
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines = []; aggregatedTcTotal = 5; no throw; log file contains WARN line mentioning the feature directory name

test_cases.title: CLI renderer outputs integration alert line as first visible line before project rows
test_cases.given: dashboard state contains integrationAlert = {severity:'warning', message:'Aitri 0.1.77 detected — Hub integration not reviewed past 0.1.76'} and 2 registered projects
test_cases.when: the CLI renderer produces its output string
test_cases.then: the string '⚠' or '[INTEGRATION]' appears at a character index before the first project name in the output; specifically the alert line occupies line index 0 or 1 of the rendered output (before any project row)
test_cases.expected_result: rendered output split by newline: lines[0] or lines[1] contains '0.1.77' AND '0.1.76'; first line containing a project name appears after the alert line

test_cases.title: CLI TC column shows aggregatedTcTotal with '(+N features)' indicator
test_cases.given: A project entry in dashboard state with testSummary.total=30, aggregatedTcTotal=91, featurePipelines array of length 2
test_cases.when: the CLI renderer renders the project row for that project in a terminal width ≥100 columns
test_cases.then: the rendered row contains the string '91' and the string '+2 features' (or '(+2 features)')
test_cases.expected_result: row string includes '91' as the TC count and '(+2' substring indicating 2 features; '30' is not used as the TC count

test_cases.title: Web IntegrationAlertBanner renders above project cards when integrationAlert is non-null
test_cases.given: React app receives dashboard JSON with integrationAlert = {severity:'warning', message:'Aitri 0.1.77 detected — Hub integration not reviewed past 0.1.76', changelogUrl:'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/blob/main/docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md'}
test_cases.then: IntegrationAlertBanner is present in the DOM with role='alert' or data-testid='integration-alert-banner'; its text content includes both '0.1.77' and '0.1.76'; an anchor element with href matching the changelogUrl is present; IntegrationAlertBanner appears before the first element with data-testid='project-card' in document order
test_cases.expected_result: DOM query '#integration-alert-banner' or '[data-testid=integration-alert-banner]' returns 1 node; it precedes the first '[data-testid=project-card]' in the DOM; text contains '0.1.77' and '0.1.76'; link href = 'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/blob/main/docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md'

test_cases.title: CLI renders no alert line when integrationAlert is null
test_cases.given: dashboard state with integrationAlert = null and 2 registered healthy projects
test_cases.when: the CLI renderer produces its output string
test_cases.then: the output does not contain the substring '[INTEGRATION]' or 'Hub integration not reviewed'
test_cases.expected_result: rendered output contains no alert line; output starts directly with the dashboard header or first project row; no blank line left in place of the absent alert

test_cases.title: Web FeatureSummarySection is absent when featurePipelines is empty
test_cases.given: React app receives a project entry with featurePipelines = [] and aggregatedTcTotal = 30
test_cases.then: no element with data-testid='feature-summary-section' exists within that ProjectCard's DOM subtree
test_cases.expected_result: DOM query within ProjectCard for '[data-testid=feature-summary-section]' returns 0 nodes; card renders without any 'Features' section

test_cases.title: Web IntegrationAlertBanner is absent when integrationAlert is null
test_cases.given: React app receives dashboard JSON with integrationAlert = null
test_cases.then: no element with data-testid='integration-alert-banner' exists in the DOM
test_cases.expected_result: DOM query '[data-testid=integration-alert-banner]' returns 0 nodes; no layout gap or empty div in place of the absent banner

test_cases.title: detectAitriVersion returns clean semver string from aitri --version output
test_cases.given: aitri --version stdout = 'v0.1.77\n' (with prefix and trailing newline, as the CLI actually outputs)
test_cases.when: detectAitriVersion() is called with a stub that returns 'v0.1.77\n'
test_cases.then: returns the string '0.1.77' (no 'v' prefix, no whitespace)
test_cases.expected_result: return value === '0.1.77'; typeof return value === 'string'

test_cases.title: detectAitriVersion parses version from multi-word stdout correctly
test_cases.given: aitri --version stdout = '⚒  Spec-Driven Development Engine  v0.1.76\n' (banner-style output)
test_cases.when: detectAitriVersion() is called with a stub returning that string
test_cases.then: returns '0.1.76'
test_cases.expected_result: return value === '0.1.76'; regex correctly extracts first N.N.N pattern from anywhere in the string

test_cases.title: detectAitriVersion returns null and logs WARN when aitri is not on PATH
test_cases.given: execFileSync('aitri', ['--version']) throws Error with code 'ENOENT'
test_cases.then: returns null; does not throw; a WARN log entry containing 'Aitri CLI version undetectable' is written to ~/.aitri-hub/logs/aitri-hub.log
test_cases.expected_result: return value === null; no exception propagates; log file contains line with 'WARN' level and 'undetectable'

test_cases.title: detectAitriVersion returns null when aitri --version times out after 3000ms
test_cases.given: execFileSync('aitri', ['--version'], {timeout:3000}) throws Error with code 'ETIMEDOUT' or 'SIGTERM'
test_cases.then: returns null; does not throw; log entry written
test_cases.expected_result: return value === null; no exception propagates to caller; subsequent collection cycle is not blocked

test_cases.title: dashboard.json written with featurePipelines and aggregatedTcTotal per project after collection
test_cases.given: A dashboard data object containing projects[0] = { name:'my-project', testSummary:{total:30}, featurePipelines:[{name:'feat-a', tcCount:61}], aggregatedTcTotal:91 } and integrationAlert = null
test_cases.when: writeDashboard(data) is called and the file is read back from disk
test_cases.then: the parsed JSON contains projects[0].featurePipelines array of length 1 with featurePipelines[0].name='feat-a' and featurePipelines[0].tcCount=61; projects[0].aggregatedTcTotal=91; integrationAlert=null
test_cases.expected_result: JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(dashboardFilePath())).projects[0].aggregatedTcTotal === 91; .featurePipelines[0].tcCount === 61

test_cases.title: dashboard.json written with top-level integrationAlert when version mismatch exists
test_cases.given: A dashboard data object with integrationAlert = {severity:'warning', message:'Aitri 0.1.77 detected — Hub integration not reviewed past 0.1.76', changelogUrl:'https://github.com/cesareyeserrano/Aitri/blob/main/docs/integrations/CHANGELOG.md'}
test_cases.when: writeDashboard(data) is called and the file is read back
test_cases.then: parsed JSON.integrationAlert.severity === 'warning'; JSON.integrationAlert.message includes '0.1.77'; JSON.integrationAlert.changelogUrl is a non-empty string; file is valid JSON (JSON.parse does not throw)
test_cases.expected_result: integrationAlert present at top-level; severity='warning'; message contains both version strings; changelogUrl non-empty

test_cases.title: dashboard.json written with featurePipelines=[] and aggregatedTcTotal=mainCount for project with no features
test_cases.given: A dashboard data object with projects[0] = { name:'no-feat-project', testSummary:{total:15}, featurePipelines:[], aggregatedTcTotal:15 }
test_cases.when: writeDashboard(data) is called and file is read back
test_cases.then: projects[0].featurePipelines is an empty array ([]) and projects[0].aggregatedTcTotal === 15
test_cases.expected_result: Array.isArray(projects[0].featurePipelines) === true; projects[0].featurePipelines.length === 0; aggregatedTcTotal === 15

test_cases.title: dashboard.json schema extension does not break existing FR-001 through FR-009 fields
test_cases.given: A full dashboard data object including all pre-existing fields: name, location, status, phases, verifyStatus, gitMeta, testSummary, alerts AND the new featurePipelines, aggregatedTcTotal, integrationAlert fields
test_cases.when: writeDashboard(data) is called and file is read back
test_cases.then: all original fields are present and unmodified: projects[0].phases.current is a number; projects[0].gitMeta.branch is a string; projects[0].alerts is an array; AND new fields coexist without overwriting existing ones
test_cases.expected_result: Backward compatibility confirmed: all existing required fields present with correct types alongside new fields

test_cases.title: Previous valid dashboard.json is preserved when a write fails mid-operation
test_cases.given: dashboard.json already contains valid data = {projects:[{name:'existing-project', aggregatedTcTotal:30}]}; a subsequent writeDashboard call fails at the fs.renameSync step (simulated by making the tmp file path unwritable)
test_cases.then: the original dashboard.json still contains {projects:[{name:'existing-project', aggregatedTcTotal:30}]}; the tmp file does not exist; no exception propagates to the caller
test_cases.expected_result: dashboard.json content unchanged from before the failing write; tmp file absent; no unhandled exception

test_cases.title: Collection cycle for 1 project with 10 features completes within 5000ms
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with 10 feature subdirectories each containing a valid .aitri file and spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json; mainTcTotal = 30
test_cases.when: readFeaturePipelines(projectDir, 30) is called and timed with Date.now()
test_cases.then: the function returns within 5000ms; featurePipelines.length = 10; aggregatedTcTotal = 30 + sum of 10 feature TC counts
test_cases.expected_result: elapsed = Date.now() - start; elapsed < 5000; featurePipelines.length === 10

test_cases.title: Malformed feature .aitri does not crash collector or affect other projects
test_cases.given: A temp project dir with 3 feature subdirectories: features/ok1 (valid .aitri, tcCount:10), features/broken (malformed .aitri = 'not-json'), features/ok2 (valid .aitri, tcCount:20); mainTcTotal = 5
test_cases.then: returns featurePipelines of length 2 (ok1 and ok2 only); aggregatedTcTotal = 35 (5+10+20); 'broken' is absent from featurePipelines; no exception thrown
test_cases.expected_result: featurePipelines.length === 2; aggregatedTcTotal === 35; names in featurePipelines include 'ok1' and 'ok2'; 'broken' absent

test_cases.title: CLI version detection failure writes WARN entry to aitri-hub.log
test_cases.given: execFileSync stub throws ENOENT; appendLog function is spied upon
test_cases.then: appendLog is called once with a string containing 'WARN' (case-insensitive) and 'undetectable'
test_cases.expected_result: spy on appendLog records exactly 1 call; call argument matches /warn/i and /undetectable/i

test_cases.title: Full collector cycle with version mismatch produces integrationAlert in dashboard.json and CLI shows alert before project rows
test_cases.given: A registered local project with a valid .aitri; INTEGRATION_LAST_REVIEWED = '0.1.76'; detectAitriVersion stubbed to return '0.1.77'; dashboard.json written to os.tmpdir()
test_cases.when: A full collection cycle runs (collector/index.js collectAll()) and the CLI renderer processes the resulting state
test_cases.then: (1) dashboard.json contains top-level integrationAlert with severity='warning' and message containing '0.1.77'; (2) CLI renderer output string has the alert line at line position 0 or 1, before any project name
test_cases.expected_result: dashboard.json.integrationAlert.severity === 'warning'; CLI output lines[0..2] contains '0.1.77'; first project name in output appears after the alert line

test_cases.title: Full collector cycle with feature sub-pipelines produces correct aggregated TC count in dashboard.json
test_cases.given: A registered local project in os.tmpdir() with: spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json {summary:{passed:30,failed:0,total:30}}; features/feat-a/.aitri = {approvedPhases:[1,2,3], currentPhase:4}; features/feat-a/spec/04_TEST_RESULTS.json = {summary:{passed:58,failed:3,total:61}}; integrationAlert = null
test_cases.when: A full collection cycle runs (collectAll()) writing to os.tmpdir()/dashboard.json
test_cases.then: dashboard.json projects[0].aggregatedTcTotal === 91; projects[0].featurePipelines.length === 1; projects[0].featurePipelines[0].name === 'feat-a'; projects[0].featurePipelines[0].tcCount === 61; projects[0].testSummary.total remains 30 (main only)
test_cases.expected_result: All 5 assertions above pass; dashboard.json is valid JSON; no exception thrown during collection
